models.model_summary: All-new 2026 compact sedan with a 149-hp 2.0L I4 and CVT, four trim levels (S, SV, SR, SL), and a new interior built around dual 12.3-inch displays. Earned IIHS Top Safety Pick+ thanks to revised ADAS suite and 10 standard airbags.

reliability.summary: All-new 9th-generation has no model-level track record yet; JD Power VDS measures 3-year-old cars. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — well below the 204 PP100 industry average and 10th in the mass-market rankings. Nissan is also in the top 10 of Consumer Reports' 2026 reliability rankings. Prior 8th-gen Sentras have shown average-to-above-average dependability historically.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the all-new 2026 Sentra.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers credit the new Sentra for a far more distinctive exterior, materially upgraded interior with standard 12.3-inch touchscreen, and IIHS-validated safety, but criticize sluggish 0-60 (~9.7 sec) and a CVT that hampers driving enthusiasm. Edmunds rates it slightly above the Corolla on roominess and tech but notes the lack of power.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient 2026 owner reviews aggregated yet; the all-new model has only been on sale since late 2025. Outgoing 8th-gen Sentras earned ~4.4 stars on Edmunds/KBB for value and ride comfort, with common complaints about CVT drone and underwhelming acceleration.

trims.notes: EPA fuel economy ID 50063 (2-mode) — corresponds to S/SV with 16-17" wheels. NHTSA had not yet rated the 2026 Sentra at research time.

features.sound_system: 6-speaker audio (8-speaker Bose with SR Premium Package)
notable_other: Sport-themed black front fascia, side sills, rear bumper, rear spoiler

trims.notes: Bose 8-speaker premium audio, leatherette upholstery, and surround-view camera available via optional SR Premium Package.

models.notes: All-new 9th-gen Sentra for 2026. Built in Aguascalientes, Mexico. IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award confirmed for 2026 MY. NHTSA has not yet posted 2026 ratings (5-star overall on 2025 model). EPA IDs: 50063 (S/SV 2-mode), 50061 (S/SV 3-mode), 50062 (SR/SL with 18" wheels). Image URLs are nissanusa.com consumer page placeholders requiring follow-up CDN extraction.

models.generation_context: 6th-gen (L34), in its final model year; production ends mid-2026
models.model_summary: Final-year midsize sedan with a 2.5L 188-hp I4, Xtronic CVT, and choice of FWD or Intelligent AWD. Streamlined for 2026 to SV and SR core trims (base S and SL dropped) plus SV Special Edition and SR Midnight Edition appearance variants.

reliability.summary: Long-running 6th gen has a mixed reliability record per Consumer Reports (about-average); JD Power VDS has not published a 2026-specific score. Common issues center on CVT noise/durability on older units.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Altima.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Altima offers a comfortable ride, available AWD, and good fuel economy for a midsize sedan, but the cabin feels dated against newer rivals and the CVT-bound 2.5L is uninspiring. With the SR adding sportier suspension and 19-inch wheels, it remains a value pick in its final year.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise fuel economy, comfortable ride, and value pricing; common complaints involve CVT drone under hard acceleration, dated infotainment on lower trims, and lack of available powertrain options.

trims.notes: EPA ID 50064. IIHS 2026 ratings: Good small overlap, Marginal moderate overlap, Poor side; no TSP award. NHTSA 5-star carries from 2025 (not yet re-tested for 2026). 2026 is final production year.

changes: Adds gloss black 17-inch wheels
Adds rear spoiler
Adds carbon-fiber-appearance trim on center console
Adds upgraded 12.3-inch touchscreen with wireless Android Auto (vs 8-inch wired)

trims.notes: Images shared with SV trim_family per spec §7.

changes: Adds Intelligent All-Wheel Drive
Engine slightly de-rated to 182 hp / 178 lb-ft for AWD calibration
Higher curb weight (~3,461 lb vs 3,209 lb)
Lower fuel economy (25/33/28 MPG vs 26/36/30 MPG)

trims.notes: EPA ID 50065. AWD adds $1,500 to FWD MSRP across all trims per Nissan press release.

notable_other: Paddle shifters with manual CVT mode

changes: Adds 19-inch alloy wheels and sport-tuned suspension
Adds paddle shifters with manual CVT mode
Adds 12.3-inch touchscreen with wireless Android Auto and wireless charging
Sport-themed exterior (rear spoiler, dark exterior trim) and sport cloth interior

trims.notes: Note: per spec §3.6 SR uses same EPA ID 50064 as SV (FWD) — wheels are different but EPA does not separately list.

changes: Adds 19-inch black alloy wheels (vs 17-inch on SV)
Adds black exterior accents (badging, mirror caps, rear spoiler)
Black-painted roof
Sport-tuned suspension and paddle shifters from SR

trims.notes: Images shared with SR trim_family per spec §7.

changes: Adds Intelligent All-Wheel Drive (engine 182 hp/178 lb-ft AWD calibration)
Adds 19-inch alloy wheels and sport-tuned suspension (from SR)
Lower fuel economy (25/33/28 MPG vs 26/36/30 MPG)

trims.notes: Images shared with SR trim_family.

models.notes: 2026 Altima is final model year before discontinuation; production ends mid-2026. Sixth-generation L34 platform unchanged. S and SL trims dropped from lineup. EPA IDs: 50064 (FWD), 50065 (AWD). IIHS 2026 ratings exist but do not earn TSP award (Marginal moderate overlap, Poor side impact). NHTSA 5-star carries from 2025 model — 2026 has not been separately tested.

models.generation_context: 7th-gen (RZ34), introduced for 2023; 2026 adds Heritage Edition and revised lineup
models.model_summary: Rear-drive two-seat sports coupe with a 400-hp 3.0L twin-turbo V6, choice of 6-speed manual or 9-speed automatic, and trims spanning Sport, Performance, and limited Heritage Edition. NISMO trim has its own model page and is catalogued separately.

reliability.summary: Niche-volume sports car with no JD Power VDS or CR predicted-reliability score at the model level. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — below the 204 PP100 industry average and 10th in mass-market rankings. Early RZ34 owner reports cite generally solid mechanicals with some early-build infotainment glitches.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the Z.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rate the Z as a properly engaging modern sports car with strong twin-turbo V6 thrust and rewarding handling, though they note dated infotainment versus rivals and CVT-free transmissions both having compromises. The 2026 Heritage Edition adds visual appeal but doesn't change the dynamic formula.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the V6's mid-range punch, manual transmission rev-match, and bold styling; common complaints are dated infotainment, road noise, and limited cargo space.

trims.notes: EPA ID 49777 (manual). 9-speed automatic version is EPA ID 49778. NHTSA/IIHS have not crash-tested the Z.

changes: Adds 9-speed automatic transmission with paddle shifters and launch control
Higher EPA fuel economy (19/28/22 vs 18/24/20)
Faster 0-60 (~4.3 sec vs 4.5 sec manual)

trims.notes: Images shared with Sport trim_family.
Performance trim available with 6-speed manual (standard) or 9-speed automatic (+$2,000).
Singleton trim_family per spec §7 — own 4 image angles required. Limited to 500 units globally.

models.notes: Sport trim with manual transmission is the base of the Z model line; Sport Automatic, Performance, and Heritage Edition are step-ups. NISMO is split as a separate model per spec §6.4 (own nissanusa.com showroom page at /sports-cars/nissan-z/nismo.html). EPA IDs: 49777 (manual), 49778 (automatic). NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested the current Z.

models.generation_context: RZ34-based NISMO variant; track-focused trim with own model page
models.model_summary: Track-focused NISMO variant of the Z with a 420-hp twin-turbo V6, automatic-only NISMO-tuned 9-speed transmission, stiffer chassis, RAYS 19-inch wheels on Dunlop tires, and unique aero. Sold as a separate model line on nissanusa.com.

reliability.summary: Very low-volume performance variant; no model-level reliability data published. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — below the 204 PP100 industry average. The Z NISMO shares its VR30DDTT twin-turbo V6 with the standard Z, with track-tuned chassis hardware.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the Z NISMO.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Z NISMO's sharper turn-in, much-improved body control over the standard Z, and 3.9-second 0-60 sprint, but note the lack of a manual transmission disappoints purists, and the price approaches more capable rivals like the Supra 3.0 and Mustang Dark Horse.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner reviews aggregated for the Z NISMO specifically.

notable_other: NISMO-tuned suspension (larger shocks, higher spring rates)
G-nose-inspired front fascia and canard vortex generators
Larger 15-inch front rotors with unique super-rigid calipers
Sport+ Drive Mode with 50% quicker downshifts
Recaro sport seats with red accents

trims.notes: Sole-trim model — is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null per spec §6.2. EPA ID 49779 confirmed for 2026 NISMO. Automatic-only — manual transmission is not offered on NISMO. 0-60 of 3.9 sec is Nissan-claimed.

models.notes: Split as separate model from base Z per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule (own nissanusa.com showroom page at /sports-cars/nissan-z/nismo.html). Sole-trim atomic rule applied. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the Z NISMO. 0-60 of 3.9 sec is manufacturer claim; Car and Driver tested ~3.9 sec.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen, all-new for 2026 — moves from hatchback to compact crossover SUV; built on CMF-EV platform shared with Ariya
models.model_summary: All-new 3rd-gen LEAF transforms from hatchback to compact electric crossover with a 75 kWh battery, up to 303 miles of EPA range, and a native NACS Tesla-style charging port (US first-ever for Nissan). Three trims: S+, SV+, Platinum+, all 75 kWh — the planned 52 kWh variant was cancelled for the US.

reliability.summary: All-new 3rd-generation platform with no model-level reliability track record. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — below the 204 PP100 industry average. Consumer Reports gave the first impression as solid but cited some early software glitches in the dual 14.3-inch display system.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the all-new 2026 LEAF.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the LEAF's transformation into a crossover with native NACS charging, 300+ mile EPA range, and dual 14.3-inch displays at a sub-$30K starting price; common critiques cite front-wheel-drive only (no AWD option), slower DC fast charging than rivals (150 kW peak), and a noticeable step up from base S+ to top trims on equipment.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient aggregated 2026 owner reviews yet — the all-new model has just begun reaching dealers.

notable_other: Native NACS DC fast-charge port + J1772 AC port

trims.notes: S+ uses 18-inch steel wheels with covers (EPA ID 49975, 131/111/121 MPGe, 303 mi range — the LEAF's range champion). NHTSA/IIHS have not yet rated the all-new 2026 LEAF. The originally planned 52 kWh entry-level S trim was cancelled for the US in early 2026.

notable_other: Heat pump for improved cold-weather range

trims.notes: EPA ID 49974 (122/105/114 MPGe, 288 mi range with 18-inch alloy wheels).
EPA ID 49976 (110/96/103 MPGe, 259 mi range with 19-inch wheels). Top trim of LEAF lineup.

models.notes: All-new 3rd-gen LEAF transitions from hatchback to compact crossover SUV, built on CMF-EV platform shared with Ariya. Native NACS charging port standard (Nissan's first US EV with NACS factory). All three US trims share the 75 kWh battery; the planned 52 kWh entry-level was cancelled for the US market. EPA IDs: 49975 (S+ 18" steel, 303 mi), 49974 (SV+ 18" alloy, 288 mi), 49976 (Platinum+ 19", 259 mi). NHTSA/IIHS have not yet rated the all-new LEAF.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen, redesigned for 2025; 2026 is carryover with minor updates
models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover with a 141-hp 2.0L I4, CVT, and class-best 60 cu ft cargo with rear seats folded. Three trims (S, SV, SR) with optional Intelligent AWD on SR; SR adds 19-inch wheels and the 12.3-inch driver display.

reliability.summary: Second generation has limited reliability data; Consumer Reports rates predicted reliability above average. JD Power has not published a specific 2026 VDS score.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Nissan model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers commend the Kicks for its affordable starting price, generous cargo space, and standard Safety Shield 360 suite; common criticisms are modest 141-hp output that struggles up grades, CVT drone, and a sparsely equipped base trim.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners appreciate the Kicks's fuel economy, ride quality, and value pricing; common complaints involve underpowered acceleration when fully loaded, road noise, and minimal standard tech on the S trim.

trims.notes: EPA ID 49845. IIHS rates Good for crashworthiness but Marginal vehicle-to-vehicle prevention — no TSP. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings.

notable_other: Leatherette-appointed seats with sport cloth inserts
Adaptive Cruise Control, lane-centering, Intelligent Around View Monitor

changes: Adds 19-inch alloy wheels (vs 16-inch steel)
Adds 12.3-inch infotainment touchscreen and 12.3-inch driver display
Adds synthetic-leather/sport-cloth seating, power driver seat, heated front seats, heated steering wheel
Adds Adaptive Cruise Control, lane-centering, 360-degree camera (Intelligent Around View)
Adds wireless charging, remote start, ambient lighting
Bose 10-speaker premium audio optional (only on SR)
Adds Intelligent All-Wheel Drive
Includes all SR FWD equipment (19-inch wheels, dual 12.3-inch displays, power/heated front seats, ACC)
Lower EPA fuel economy (27/34/30 MPG vs 28/35/31 MPG FWD)
Larger 12.4-gal fuel tank for AWD

trims.notes: EPA ID 49868. Images shared with SR trim_family.

models.notes: 2nd-gen Kicks redesigned for MY25; 2026 is carryover. EPA IDs: 49845 (FWD), 49868 (AWD). Destination fee is $1,545 for Kicks (different from sedan $1,245). IIHS rates Good crashworthiness with Marginal V2V crash prevention — no TSP award. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen (T33), mid-cycle enhancement marketed as 2026.5; new Dark Armor grade added
models.model_summary: Compact crossover SUV with a 1.5L VC-turbo 3-cylinder (201 hp), 8-speed automatic, and choice of FWD or Intelligent AWD across six trims (S, SV, Dark Armor, Rock Creek, SL, Platinum). 2026.5 mid-cycle update adds Dark Armor grade and refreshed styling.

reliability.summary: Mid-cycle refresh of the third generation; Consumer Reports rates predicted reliability as average. JD Power has not published a 2026-specific VDS score.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Nissan model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the refreshed Rogue for its solid ride and handling, premium-feeling interior with 12.3-inch touchscreen, and class-competitive efficiency from the 1.5L VC-turbo three-cylinder. Critiques cite uninspiring acceleration, occasional CVT-like behavior despite 8-speed designation, and increased pricing for 2026.5.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners commend ride comfort, fuel economy, and cargo space; common complaints are engine noise under acceleration, infotainment lag, and modest acceleration from the three-cylinder.

trims.notes: EPA ID 49464 (FWD). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick (not TSP+). NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and front seats
Gloss black exterior accents (mirrors, badges, grille bars, pillar trim)
HD Intelligent Around View Monitor with Moving Object Detection

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family (Dark Armor exclusive styling). New for 2026.5 mid-cycle refresh.

notable_other: Unique off-road front fascia and grille
Rugged exterior cladding and orange accents

trims.notes: EPA ID 49525 (AWD Rock Creek). Singleton trim_family (unique off-road styling).
EPA ID 49524 (AWD). Top trim.

models.notes: 2026.5 mid-cycle enhancement adds Dark Armor grade and refreshed styling. Built in Smyrna, TN. EPA IDs: 49464 (FWD), 49524 (AWD), 49525 (AWD Rock Creek). IIHS 2026 Top Safety Pick (not TSP+) for Rogue. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings. Pricing per Nissan's July 2025 press release for 2026.5 starting MSRP $29,490 (S FWD). All trims can add AWD for $1,400. Hybrid e-POWER variant arrives late 2026 — not included here as it is post-research-date.

models.generation_context: All-new 2026 model; Rogue PHEV is a US-rebranded Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV (alliance partner)
models.model_summary: All-new plug-in hybrid SUV with 248-hp powertrain (2.4L I4 + dual electric motors), 20-kWh battery, 38-mile EPA all-electric range, and 420-mile total range. Standard Intelligent AWD; available in SL and Platinum trims with optional three-row 7-seat configuration.

reliability.summary: All-new 2026 PHEV variant; underlying Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV platform has shown average reliability per CR. No model-specific 2026 Rogue PHEV scores published. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — below the 204 PP100 industry average and 10th in mass-market rankings.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the all-new 2026 Rogue Plug-in Hybrid.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Rogue PHEV brings respectable 38-mile electric range and standard AWD to Nissan's midsize lineup, with a 7-seat option being a class rarity, but flag that the platform is shared with the Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV and feels less refined than newer PHEV competitors.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient aggregated owner reviews for the all-new 2026 model.

powertrain.transmission: single-speed direct drive (front motor) + CVT-like operation

notable_other: 20 kWh battery, 38 mi EV range, 420 mi total range
Standard AWD with seven drive modes

trims.notes: EPA has not yet published a specific 2026 Rogue PHEV entry as of 2026-05-12; source set to EPA brand model-browse page per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. PHEV is the only powertrain offered (no ICE Rogue PHEV mismatch). Built on Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV platform per Nissan-Mitsubishi alliance. NHTSA/IIHS have not rated the 2026 PHEV. Charge-sustaining MPG values (26/27/26) per Nissan published estimates pending EPA verification.

models.notes: All-new 2026 Rogue PHEV; first plug-in hybrid Nissan offers in the US. Platform shared with Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV under the Nissan-Mitsubishi alliance. EPA has not yet published 2026 entries; brand model-browse page used per spec §4 fallback rule. Standard AWD across all trims. 10yr/100k EV battery warranty per Nissan PHEV terms. NHTSA/IIHS have not rated the 2026 model. Hybrid line is single — both trims share same powertrain; SL is the base of the only powertrain line.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen, fully redesigned for 2025; 2026 makes AWD standard and adds SL Comfort Package
models.model_summary: Midsize two-row crossover with a 2.0L VC-Turbo I4 (241 hp), 9-speed automatic, and now-standard Intelligent AWD. Three trims (SV, SL, Platinum) with a new SL Comfort Package adding ventilated massaging seats and hands-free liftgate.

reliability.summary: Second model year of all-new 4th-gen platform; Consumer Reports rated the 2025 model below average on early predicted reliability due to new VC-Turbo powertrain. JD Power has not published a 2026 VDS score.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Nissan model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned Murano's striking exterior, premium-leaning interior with 12.3-inch dual displays, and refined ride; common critiques cite the modest 241-hp VC-Turbo replacing the prior V6, increased pricing now that AWD is standard, and lack of a hybrid option.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners commend the new Murano's quiet cabin, ride comfort, and premium-feeling interior; common complaints involve lack of V6 power (vs prior gen), tight rear cargo, and infotainment learning curve.

trims.notes: EPA ID 49867 (only AWD listed). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+. AWD now standard for 2026 (FWD SV dropped). NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings.

notable_other: SL Comfort Package available (ventilated massaging seats, heated rear, motion-activated liftgate)

models.notes: 4th-gen Murano redesigned for 2025; 2026 makes AWD standard across the lineup (FWD SV dropped) and adds SL Comfort Package. EPA ID 49867 (only AWD entry). IIHS 2026 Top Safety Pick+ confirmed. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings. 2.0L VC-Turbo replaces the prior 3.5L V6.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen (R53), introduced 2022; carryover with Rock Creek refresh
models.model_summary: Three-row 8-seat midsize SUV with a 3.5L V6 (284 hp; 295 hp in Rock Creek), 9-speed automatic, and choice of 2WD or 4WD. Four trims: SV, Rock Creek, SL, Platinum. Class-leading 6,000-lb tow rating on Rock Creek, Platinum, and SV/SL with Premium Package.

reliability.summary: Fifth-gen Pathfinder has shown above-average dependability per JD Power's 2024 study (model-level data not yet specific to 2026). CR rates predicted reliability average.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Nissan model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Pathfinder's traditional naturally-aspirated V6 power, premium-leaning Platinum trim, and class-competitive 6,000-lb tow rating, but note that the third-row space is tight for adults and fuel economy lags hybrid rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the V6 powertrain, towing capability, and family-friendly cabin; common complaints are tight third-row legroom and mediocre fuel economy.

trims.notes: EPA ID 50146 (2WD). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+. Standard 6,000-lb tow with optional Premium Package; base 3,500 lb. 4WD optional (+$2,000).

notable_other: Exclusive Rock Creek leatherette seating with orange contrast stitching

trims.notes: EPA ID 50175 (4WD Rock Creek). Singleton trim_family (off-road-themed unique styling). Premium fuel required for 295-hp tune.

notable_other: Available Premium Package adds 6,000-lb tow, hands-free liftgate, 13-speaker Bose

trims.notes: EPA ID 50174 (4WD Platinum). FWD configuration also available at lower price.

models.notes: 5th-gen Pathfinder (R53) introduced 2022; 2026 is carryover with minor updates. EPA IDs: 50146 (2WD), 50173 (4WD), 50174 (4WD Platinum), 50175 (4WD Rock Creek). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ confirmed. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings. Standard 3,500-lb tow; 6,000-lb tow standard on Rock Creek and Platinum, optional on SV/SL with Premium Package. Rock Creek splits as trim within Pathfinder (not separate model) per spec — does not have its own model page on nissanusa.com.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen, all-new for 2025 (Y63 platform); 2026 adds NISMO and Platinum Reserve trims
models.model_summary: Full-size body-on-frame 8-seat SUV with all-new 3.5L twin-turbo V6 (425 hp; 460 hp in NISMO), 9-speed automatic, and choice of RWD or 4WD. Six trims for 2026: SV, SL, Platinum, PRO-4X, Platinum Reserve, and new NISMO (catalogued separately).

reliability.summary: Second model year of the all-new Y63 platform; insufficient model-level track record. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — below the 204 PP100 industry average and 10th in mass-market rankings. No model-specific 2026 VDS for Armada was published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the 2026 Armada.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the redesigned Armada's twin-turbo V6 replacement of the prior 5.6L V8, modern interior with dual screens, and competitive towing/payload, but flag fuel economy that lags hybrid competitors and pricing that creeps into Lincoln Navigator territory at the top.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners commend the powerful twin-turbo V6, spacious cabin, and modern technology; common complaints involve fuel economy and infotainment quirks in the new system.

trims.notes: EPA ID 49557 (2WD). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick. 4WD optional ($3,000). Destination fee $1,995 (full-size SUVs).
EPA ID 49603 (4WD PRO-4X). Singleton trim_family per off-road styling.
Singleton trim_family per spec §7 — own 4 image angles required.

models.notes: 3rd-gen Armada all-new for 2025; 2026 adds NISMO (split as separate model per spec §6.4) and new Platinum Reserve. EPA IDs: 49557 (2WD), 49602 (4WD), 49603 (4WD PRO-4X). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings. Destination fee $1,995 for Armada. 4WD optional on 2WD trims for $3,000. PRO-4X and NISMO are 4WD-only. NISMO catalogued separately per Nissan's dedicated /armada/nismo.html showroom page.

models.generation_context: First-ever NISMO Armada; debuts 2026 as track-inspired performance variant of 3rd-gen Armada
models.model_summary: First-ever NISMO version of the Armada — a 460-hp tuned 3.5L twin-turbo V6 full-size SUV with 22-inch RAYS forged wheels, performance tires, adaptive air suspension, and sport seats with massage. Sole-trim model.

reliability.summary: All-new performance variant with no model-level track record yet. Brand-level Nissan scored 194 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — below the 204 PP100 industry average. The Armada NISMO shares the standard Armada's Y63 twin-turbo V6 platform with performance-tuned chassis hardware.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score is published for the Armada NISMO.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Armada NISMO is a novel concept — Nissan's first attempt at a performance-focused full-size SUV — with 460 hp, adaptive air suspension, and aggressive 22-inch RAYS forged wheels, though some question whether the formula justifies the near-$80K starting price versus rivals like the Cadillac Escalade-V.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner reviews aggregated for the first-year Armada NISMO.

wheels_tires.tire_spec: 295/45R22 NISMO RAYS forged alloy with performance tires

notable_other: 22-inch NISMO RAYS forged-alloy wheels with performance tires
Revised V6 tuning (460 hp on premium fuel)
NISMO sport seats with massage function
Unique aggressive front fascia and rear styling
NissanConnect 14.3-inch display with Services and Google built-in

trims.notes: Sole-trim model — is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null per spec §6.2. EPA ID 49603 shared with standard Armada PRO-4X (NISMO does not have separately published EPA entry for 2026 — fuel economy values are PRO-4X 4WD baseline since the NISMO uses the same drivetrain). 0-60 of 5.6 sec is manufacturer claim. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the NISMO.

models.notes: Split as separate model from base Armada per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule (own nissanusa.com showroom page at /armada/nismo.html). Sole-trim atomic rule applied. EPA has not published a NISMO-specific 2026 listing — fuel economy values reflect the PRO-4X 4WD baseline since NISMO uses the same 4WD drivetrain (Nissan does not separately publish MPG for NISMO; reported as 'comparable to PRO-4X' in press materials).

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen (D41), introduced 2022; 2026 expands trim lineup and adds SL trim
models.model_summary: Midsize pickup with a 3.8L V6 (310 hp), 9-speed automatic, and choice of King Cab or Crew Cab with 5- or 6-foot bed. Five trims for 2026: S, SV, PRO-X (4x2 off-road), PRO-4X (4x4 off-road), and SL (luxury). 7,150-lb max tow rating.

reliability.summary: Third-gen Frontier has shown average reliability per Consumer Reports. JD Power has not published 2026-specific scores.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for this 2026 Nissan model.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Frontier's standard 310-hp V6, rugged styling, and capable off-road PRO-4X trim, but note that the Crew Cab's rear-seat legroom trails competitors, the V6's fuel economy is mediocre, and the interior materials feel basic on lower trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners commend the V6 powertrain, ride quality, and durable build; common complaints are tight rear-cab space (King Cab) and lower-trim interior quality.

notable_other: Cloth seats with rear jump seats

trims.notes: EPA ID 49833 (2WD). King Cab body with 5-ft bed. IIHS rates Good moderate overlap front and Acceptable side; no TSP. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings. Destination fee $1,795 for Frontier.

notable_other: Crew Cab body with 5-ft or 6-ft bed available
Blind Spot Warning and Rear Cross Traffic Alert

trims.notes: Crew Cab body. SV with 6-ft bed is $36,690.
PRO-X is the 4x2 (RWD) off-road trim — distinct from the 4x4 PRO-4X. Singleton trim_family.

notable_other: Skid plates and red tow hooks
Available as Crew Cab 5-ft bed ($41,870) or 6-ft bed ($42,370)

trims.notes: EPA ID 49839 (4WD PRO-4X). Singleton trim_family per off-road styling. PRO-4X 6-ft long bed is $42,370.
New SL trim for 2026 (luxury). Pricing $42,370 is for 4x2 configuration; 4x4 adds approximately $3,000.

models.notes: 3rd-gen Frontier (D41) introduced 2022; 2026 expands trim lineup with new SL trim and adds 6-ft long bed availability on more trims. EPA IDs: 49833 (2WD), 49838 (4WD), 49839 (4WD PRO-4X). IIHS rates Good moderate overlap and Acceptable side; no TSP. NHTSA has not posted 2026 ratings. Destination fee $1,795 for Frontier. Bed dimensions extended via custom bed_length_in/bed_volume_cuft per spec for trucks. PRO-X (4x2 off-road) and PRO-4X (4x4 off-road) are different trims, not separate models.
